title: kdc site record
description: A resolved data-center project-site record, built by clustering signals. Every value must trace to a source; see docs/founding/schema-sketch.md.

id.description: Unique slug; must equal the filename stem.

operator.description: Ref: data/operators.yml id. Omit when no end operator is known (undisclosed campuses).

developer.description: Ref: data/operators.yml id, when distinct from operator.

status.description: Dominant lifecycle stage; phase mixes go in notes. cancelled covers denied/withdrawn/abandoned — the distinction stays in notes until it clusters (ruling: platform#52). Evolving the enum is a schema-promotion decision (orange issue).

country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code (US, GB, IE, …).

state.description: US: 2-letter USPS code (required for US sites — enforced below). Non-US: omit; use county/locality (or region) for the sub-national area.

region.description: Non-US first-level subdivision (province / land / nation-within-UK) when relevant and not captured by county/locality.

county.description: County, parish, independent city, or non-US equivalent.

parcel_ids.description: Jurisdiction parcel/GPIN identifiers, only when found in a source. Geometry: data/parcels/<id>.geojson (deferred).

allOf.$comment: US sites keep the strict 2-letter USPS state (required); non-US sites omit state and locate via region/county/locality.

metrics.description: Evolving section — promote a new attribute only via an orange needs-human issue (see CLAUDE.md).

capacity_mw_basis.description: What the MW figure measures, when sources say.

water.description: Free-form until a measurable cluster promotes it.

first_seen.description: When kdc first recorded this site.

confidence.description: Aggregate corroboration level across clustered signals.

signals.description: Refs: data/signals/<id>.yml — the observations this record resolves.

notes.description: Conflicting reports, phase detail, unit ambiguity — the signal texture.

fuzzyDate.description: YYYY, YYYY-MM, or YYYY-MM-DD — as precise as sources allow.
